assessment:
The Issue #740 diff (b9f9d21d) is a focused, already-verified bug fix that restores AutoYesToggle in each PC per-split footer with per-CLI toggling. After reviewing only the diff, the implementation is clean, behavior-preserving, and well-documented. No safe, clearly-beneficial, low-risk improvement was found, so the correct outcome is no-change.

(1) makeAutoYesToggleHandler(paneCli) inside renderSplitPane produces a fresh closure each render. This was assessed and intentionally LEFT AS-IS. renderSplitPane already creates inline arrows every render (onCliToolChange={(id)=>{...}} at WorktreeDetailRefactored.tsx:1486 and onInsertConsumed={()=>...} at :1494), so TerminalSplitPaneContent's React.memo was already defeated across parent re-renders (which occur every poll) BEFORE #740. Memoizing only onAutoYesToggle would NOT restore any memo benefit because those pre-existing inline arrows would still yield fresh references each render; it would add a per-CLI handler cache (complexity) for zero practical gain. Actually fixing the churn would require memoizing onCliToolChange + onInsertConsumed + onAutoYesToggle together — a refactor of pre-existing code that is explicitly out of scope. The factory itself is already correctly stable (useCallback, dep [worktreeId]; it reads activeCliTabRef.current rather than activeCliTab, consistent with the existing ref pattern at :257-258 / :473 / :502).

(2) JSDoc accuracy: VERIFIED ACCURATE. The new props on TerminalSplitPaneContentProps (autoYesEnabled, autoYesExpiresAt, lastAutoResponse, onAutoYesToggle) and the updated auto-yes note (L56-85 area) correctly describe behavior. The header note at L6-7 (AutoYesToggle per-split keyed by cliToolId) is correct. Every prop the JSDoc references on AutoYesToggle (enabled, expiresAt, onToggle, lastAutoResponse, cliToolName, inline) exists on AutoYesToggleProps with matching types, and the call site (L189-197) wires them correctly. The note that lastAutoResponse stays activeCliTab-scoped and that per-split client-side auto-response is intentionally not introduced (Issue #501 server poller owns it) matches the actual implementation (useAutoYes is not called inside the component).

(3) Duplication / naming: No new duplication introduced — the curried factory REMOVES duplication by letting Mobile reuse the same implementation via the thin handleAutoYesToggle = makeAutoYesToggleHandler(activeCliTab) wrapper. Naming (makeAutoYesToggleHandler, paneAutoYes, paneAutoYesEnabled, paneAutoYesExpiresAt) is clear and consistent with surrounding conventions. The refinement that prevAutoYesEnabledRef.current is updated only when cliToolId === activeCliTabRef.current (preserving Issue #314 stop-reason toast scope) is correct and well-commented.

checks_after.target_tests: not re-run (no change made; acceptance verified 9/9 passing on b9f9d21d)
checks_after.tsc: not re-run (no change made; acceptance verified 0 errors on b9f9d21d)
checks_after.lint: not re-run (no change made; acceptance verified clean on b9f9d21d)

deferred_suggestions: Pre-existing memo churn: renderSplitPane creates fresh inline arrows (onCliToolChange, onInsertConsumed) plus makeAutoYesToggleHandler(paneCli) each render, so TerminalSplitPaneContent's React.memo provides no benefit across parent re-renders. Restoring memo would require memoizing all per-split handlers together (a pre-existing concern unrelated to #740 and explicitly out of scope). Not impactful given useTerminalPanePolling already isolates polling per pane.
Out of scope per constraints: useAutoYes refactor, non-active-split server-side auto-yes sync (expiry/stop_pattern), and AutoYesToggle redesign — intentionally not touched.
